examples: Drink water so you don't get dehydrated.

context: Let's do this. This is the hard part. My legs are shaking, but I'm trying to keep them nice and straight and low to the ground. That workout really hurt my ego. I got to chug some water after that. I'm so dehydrated. I worked up an appetite, so now it's time to cook my favorite meal of the day breakfast. I'm going to make the exact same meal that they actually eat in squid games. I'm turning on the gas. I'm pouring some oil into the pan and I'll let that heat up for a little bit. I usually use avocado oil. I'm cracking my egg and being careful not to get any pieces of the egg shell in it, which I failed at. I have a small piece. Now I have to fish it out.

examples: Your pronunciation has really improved.
English pronunciation can be tricky.
Focus on pronunciation, not just grammar.
context: Good morning, everybody! Today, I'm going to show you my daily routine, but Squid Game is themed. In this vlog, you'll learn new vocabulary words and natural English expressions that you can use in your everyday life. I recommend shadowing me as you watch to help improve your speaking and your pronunciation. I love starting my day off with a workout and I found a really interesting workout video on YouTube called Squid Gains. Gains is kind of like gym slang. It basically means muscle. So you can say something like, gotta get them gains. Or, ooh, she got gains. It means they're really fit and muscular.
